title: Domain
description: A Domain is a bounded-context wrapper that organizes related noun primitives (Resources, Persons, Roles, Groups) into a logical boundary. Domains can be multiple levels deep — e.g. organization → domain → subdomain — with primitives at the leaves.

name.description: The lowercase identifier for this domain segment.

description.description: A human-readable explanation of what this domain represents.

path.description: The fully qualified dot-separated path to this domain from the root.

domains.description: Child domains nested within this domain.

resources.description: The Resources (entity templates) that live directly within this domain.

persons.description: The Person templates (kinds of individuals) that live directly within this domain.

roles.description: The Role templates (positions/capacities) that live directly within this domain.

groups.description: The Group templates (work-units / containers) that live directly within this domain.
